His Combine was mediocre, running a 4.61 40-yard dash with pedestrian numbers across the board. Gordon was unspectacular on 70 rookie-year carries behind De'Von Achane. Now, he'll break in a new coaching staff.

Brother of Trey Lance, Bryce posted two straight 1,000-yard seasons and even caught 17 TDs in 2024. At 6'3, 210 pounds, Lance has the size and leaping ability to dominate at the FCS level. He certainly carries his brother's athleticism, but the level of competition brings major questions. Lance lasted until Day 3, but the Saints are a good spot for him to make the roster. He could ascend to the No. 3 WR behind Chris Olave and Jordyn Tyson.

Lane took a back seat to teammate Makai Lemon, but don't overlook the production here. He went 49-745-4 in 2025, following up a 12 TD season in 2024. The 6'4, 195-pound WR explodes off the line and uses his length to beat the press. Lane got Day 2 draft capital with a new Ravens coaching staff. They also drafted Elijah Sarratt though. There is an opening for Lane to emerge as a starter for the Ravens.

Benson entered the league with plenty of tread on his tires after tallying 156 and 154 carries over his final two college seasons, but a healthy James Conner limited the rookie’s impact. Then in 2025, Benson missed all but 4 games with a knee injury. He won't be guaranteed a roster spot in 2026 under a new coaching staff. Arizona added top-five pick Jeremiyah Love and veteran Tyler Allgeier in free agency.

Mitchell was traded from the Colts to the Jets and immediately made an impact on the weak WR unit. He finished the season with 33-453-2 and may have played his way into a role with the Jets in 2026. He's worth rostering as a dynasty stash. The former Longhorn turns just 24 in October.

Sarratt flashed in 2024 going 53-957-8. He missed a few games due to a hamstring injury, but he did score 13 TDs in 2025 with Fernando Mendoza at QB. Sarratt has high-end size at 6'2, 213 pounds. His high pointing is among the best in the class. The Ravens drafted WR Ja'Kobi Lane over Sarratt, but they will likely compete for targets as rookies.

Klubnik came into college football as a five-star recruit who became a starter before the end of his first season. He never reached his potential, especially in terms of developing more arm strength. He flashed some athleticism in 2024, going for 119-463-7 on the ground. He could be a late-round draft pick with some upside.

Mooney took a step back in 2025 going 32-443-1 following up his nearly 1,000 yard season in 2024. Mooney battled injuries throughout the season, but he clearly lost a step in his age-28 season. The Falcons may address the WR position this offseason pushing Mooney further down the depth chart.

Bell transferred to UConn in 2024 and broke out. Over the past two seasons, he caught 151-2138-18. He isn't built like an NFL WR, but the production is hard to ignore with Bell able to beat man coverage. He lands in a great situation with Josh Allen as a potential No. 3 WR on the Bills.

Diggs had a resurgence with the Patriots and Drake Maye in 2025, dropping 85-1013-4 in his age-32 season. But he was released by the team over the winter, as his cap figure and pending legal case sparked the move. He may not sign until the summer.

TeSlaa played a unique role in 2025, often serving as a key red zone option. He finished his rookie season going 16-239-6. His playing time increased late in the year and could continue rising in 2026 and beyond. However, he'll be limited by Amon-Ra St. Brown and Jameson Williams for the foreseeable future.

Randall came to Clemson as a WR, but transitioned to RB before 2025. The 6'2, 235 back was a track star in high school, so his size and speed offers a unique runner who succeeded on a weak Clemson offense. Randall could fill the Keaton Mitchell role down the line as a change of pace receiving back.

Thompson played at Oklahoma and Texas early in his career, but barely saw the field. He made the jump to Mississippi State in 2025 and produced, going 57-1054-6 on the season. Thompson is a big play threat who could challenge to be the fastest WR at the NFL Combine this year. Thompson is pretty blocked on the Chargers depth chart, but he may provide an occasional deep threat.

In 2025, Parkinson had a strong second half as a red zone target for Matthew Stafford. But the TE will be in a crowded situation next season as he competes with Terrance Ferguson for playing time. Parkinson is under contract for one more season.

Milroe exited college with one of the best QB rushing profiles since Lamar Jackson. He'll be developed as a passer behind Sam Darnold, who excelled as the 2025 starter. If this staff can figure out how to harness Milroe’s passing, he could be gangbusters for fantasy in a few years.

Wilson saw a surprising 103 carries in 2024, posting 4.9 YPC. His workload rose in '25, largely because of missed games from Josh Jacobs. Wilson hit the spring as a free agent and signed with a Seahawks team that just lost Kenneth Walker to the Chiefs. Zach Charbonnet is also coming off a January ACL tear. Then came the NFL Draft, when serious competition arrived in Round 1 pick Jadarian Price. The rookie puts a major dent into Wilson's dynasty value.

James is a young and explosive runner who is an ideal zone scheme fit. He posted 1,267 rushing yards in his final Oregon season in a split backfield and then went in Round 5 of the 2025 draft. He didn't see a single touch as a rookie, though. There's only an outside chance he's San Francisco's RB2 in 2026.

Black split the backfield at Indiana in 2025 with Roman Hemby, but did still go 186-1040-10 with 5.5 yards per carry. Despite not being invited to the NFL Combine, he was the third RB selected in the 2026 NFL Draft. Black will get the opportunity to compete for the backup RB job to Christian McCaffrey, but still lacks fantasy upside to become a difference maker.

Williams did very little in his rookie season, but there were flashes of being a great deep target. Williams went 10-209-3 in 2025 and should earn a larger role in 2026. But, the Patriots will likely address the position further with young WRs to compete with Williams.

Claiborne injured his ribs in the first game of the year that seemed to affect him throughout the season. He is a smaller runner with 4.3 speed who can break long runs. He has 21 rushing TDs over the last two years on a weak Wake Forest offense. Claiborne could certainly make the Vikings roster as the third RB and make an impact in 2027.

Klare broke out at Purdue in 2024 with 51-685-4. He took a step back on the Buckeyes last year at 43-448-2, but he showed improved blocking and good route running to be a shallow target. He did have some drops and mental lapses, but Klare can be a Jake Ferguson-type TE. He will split time with Terrance Ferguson, but the rest of the Rams TEs are set to become FAs within the next two years.

Mims Jr. fell behind Troy Franklin and Pat Bryan in the WR pecking order. He went 37-322-1, but Mims is still an elite returner who will stick on the roster. He just won't have the fantasy impact that was promised when he was drafted.

Fields excelled at Virginia with back-to-back 800-yard receiving seasons. He transferred to Notre Dame in 2025, and while he continued to showcase his size and contested ability, the production ultimately dropped. He's also on the older side, hitting 24 before the start of the '26 season. There certainly is an opening for Fields to start for the Giants, but he is unlikely to be a consistent fantasy threat.

Delp is an H-Back who continues in the line of great Georgia TEs. He went 20-261-1 and was used as a lead blocker often throughout the season. That blocking may get him drafted higher than his production indicates, but Delp's soft hands could be enough to become a better pro than college player. Delp could develop into the starting TE for the Saints. The team has a potential out for Juwan Johnson after this year.

Neal broke out as a true freshman on a terrible Kansas team, rushing for 707 yards and eight TDs. Since then, he tallied three straight 1,000-yard seasons, including 1,266 yards and 16 TDs in 2024. He also caught at least 20 passes in each of those three seasons. As a rookie, Neal looked just OK in late-season work with Alvin Kamara sidelined. New Orleans then added a clear-cut RB1 in Travis Etienne.

Taylor saw playing time in his rookie season as the de facto starter for the Jets, although he only mustered 44-369-1. Neck and ankle injuries caused miss games during the season, but his playing time stayed high. While Geno Smith arrived to improve the QB spot, this passing game still profiles as a bottom-five unit.

Douglas is a reliable possession WR at 6'4, 205 pounds. He went 54-846-7 last year at Texas Tech as a reliable option in the medium to deep range. His big extension allows for contested catches despite Douglas not having the athleticism to get much separation. Douglas was overdrafted on Day 2, but the WR depth chart is wide open to make an impact on the Dolphins.

Vidal is short but stout at 5’8, 215 pounds. He posted eye-popping production at Troy that includes 2,793 rushing yards and 24 TDs over his final two seasons. After a quiet 2024, injuries to Najee Harris and Omarion Hampton opened the door for Vidal to post five games of 100+ yards. He'll slide back to a reserve role in '26.

Johnson's rookie year proved disastrous. After arriving in Round 3, the Iowa standout spread only 28 carries (for 69 yards) across 10 games. He simply wasn't able to earn a role alongside Jaylen Warren and Kenneth Gainwell. We then saw how little the team believes in Johnson, as Rico Dowdle arrived on a two-year deal. Note that Warren is signed through the 2027 season.

Engram had high hopes for 2025, but he fell short of expectations (50-461-1). He now heads into his age-32 season in an offense that's suddenly stacked at WR. We're not banking on a 2026 rebound here.
Dotson went 18-262-1 on the Eagles in 2025 and still isn't making a fantasy impact. We are unlikely to see him emerge in 2026.

Joly has been a consistent producer, with three straight seasons over 480 yards receiving. He continued thriving after transferring up from UConn to NC State. Joly fell to Day 3, but he has the ability to play HC Sean Payton's "Joker" role on the team after Evan Engram leaves. He has intriguing receiving upside.

An AC Joint sprain to CeeDee Lamb caused Flournoy to get some playing time and take advnatage. He ended 2025 going 40-475-5, but flashed great hands and route running. He should stick on the Cowboys roster and could be among the top three WRs on the team in 2026 and beyond.
In 2025, Shipley played a minor role behind Saquon Barkley, who didn't miss a game due to injury. Barkley and Tank Bigsby remain under contract for '26, limiting Shipley's dynasty appeal.

Kamara set a career low in rushing yards in 2023 (694). He signed a two-year contract extension in October 2024, keeping him under team control through the 2026 season. In '24, he generally outperformed expectations before a late-season groin injury. Another injury surfaced in 2025 -- an MCL sprain -- forcing multiple absences. Now with Travis Etienne in town, Kamara's likely to play elsewhere in the fall.
